page: Vultr voc-c-2c-4gb-75s-amd is an entry-level virtual server belonging to the Optimized Cloud Compute family. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC processor running at 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 2 vCPUs mapped to 1 physical core with 2 threads per core under a shared CPU allocation model. It is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB per core, and includes 75 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. No GPU hardware is present on this instance.

Performance benchmarks reveal a divergence between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core CPU performance measured via stress-ng is strong, placing in the top 25% with a score of 2929.97. However, multi-core performance is weak, scoring 5812.19. Memory bandwidth is generally constrained, with read-only small block cached bandwidth at 267.12 GB/sec and large block uncached read bandwidth at 38.07 GB/sec, both falling into the bottom 25%. Large block write bandwidth is average at 34.24 GB/sec. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also perform at average levels. Database operations and static web serving throughput are poor, scoring in the bottom 10% of tested systems.

Given its shared CPU allocation and modest memory capacity, this server offers a highly cost-efficient profile for low-intensity workloads that do not require sustained multi-core performance. The inclusion of 75 GB of fast local NVMe SSD storage provides a solid storage foundation for basic operations. It is best suited for lightweight applications, single-threaded tasks, basic development environments, and simple background utility scripts rather than high-throughput databases or intensive web serving.
description: Vultr voc-c-2c-4gb-75s-amd is an entry-level virtual server in the Optimized Cloud Compute family, utilizing an AMD EPYC x86_64 processor. It features 2 shared vCPUs derived from 1 physical core, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 75 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU performance, scoring in the top 25% on stress-ng, but weak multi-core and memory bandwidth results. Database operations and static web serving throughput are poor, placing in the bottom 10%. Consequently, this server is highly cost-efficient for lightweight, single-threaded tasks, basic development environments, and utility scripts, but is not recommended for high-throughput database hosting or intensive web traffic.
og_description: Vultr voc-c-2c-4gb-75s-amd is an entry-level virtual server featuring 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 75 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, optimized for lightweight single-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Vultr voc-c-2c-4gb-75s-amd is an entry-level virtual server with 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 75 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A budget-friendly virtual server featuring shared AMD EPYC processors and local NVMe storage for lightweight, single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC x86_64 processor with 2 shared vCPUs and 16 MB L3 cache
4.0 GB of system memory paired with 75 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU performance in the top 25% of benchmarks
Cost-effective resource profile for non-intensive compute needs
Best suited for lightweight utility scripts and basic development environments
